_comment: Curated model-provider registry for Urfael. The brain is the `claude` CLI, which any Anthropic-Messages-API endpoint can back via ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, so 'first-class multi-model' means managing those endpoints like connectors, not hosting weights. The flat-rate Claude SUBSCRIPTION is the default (id=claude) and the thesis; every non-Anthropic row runs on YOUR own key or YOUR own hardware. `urfael model use <id>` switches provider; `urfael model opus|sonnet` pins the tier WITHIN the active provider; `urfael model route --for cost|speed|quality|privacy` recommends the best provider for a priority. Point URFAEL_PROVIDERS_INDEX at your own index.json (same schema).
_schema: id, label, kind(anthropic|openai|gemini|ollama|bedrock|vertex), baseUrl(https|loopback|empty=native cloud), big_model, small_model, models[{id,role,ctx,tools,cost,speed,quality}], authEnv(env var NAME only), authLabel, authKind(none|key|local|awschain|oauth), localToken, proxy(none|router|litellm), proxyHint, note, verified, cost, speed, quality(indicative 1-5 routing tiers), flatRate, fallbacks([provider ids to try if this one fails, in order])
_honesty: A local 7B-70B model is NOT Claude: weaker reasoning/code, shorter effective context, slower on consumer GPUs. Proxy/registry REACH is not hosting; Urfael runs no inference and brokers no aggregator's model count. OpenRouter and the proxy rows (ccr/litellm) each reach hundreds of models on your own key, so total reach is large, but the count is reach, not weights we serve. A proprietary model with no public Anthropic-format endpoint is deliberately absent, never padded. cost/speed/quality are INDICATIVE 1-5 tiers for `urfael model route`, not benchmarks; they guide a Pareto recommendation, not a guarantee. verified:false = the endpoint/models were not pinned at research time; the preview shows the exact target before any write.

providers.label: Claude (flat-rate subscription)
providers.note: The default. Your Claude Code subscription, native cloud, $0 marginal for a subscriber (per-token it is premium). Tier pin uses Claude Code aliases.

providers.label: Claude on AWS Bedrock
providers.note: Claude models billed to your AWS account; uses the AWS credential chain / region.

providers.label: Claude on Google Vertex
providers.note: Claude models billed to your GCP project; uses GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS / region.

providers.label: OpenAI (your key)
providers.proxyHint: GPT speaks the OpenAI API; needs a translating proxy (claude-code-router / LiteLLM) to back the Anthropic-shaped brain.
providers.note: Frontier GPT models on your OpenAI bill.

providers.label: Azure OpenAI (your resource)
providers.proxyHint: set your resource host + deployment; via a translating proxy.
providers.note: GPT on your Azure subscription, enterprise auth + data terms.

providers.label: Google Gemini (your key)
providers.proxyHint: Google's OpenAI-compatible endpoint; via a translating proxy. Free tier available.
providers.note: Gemini on your Google AI Studio key; long context.

providers.label: xAI Grok (your key)
providers.proxyHint: OpenAI-shaped; via a translating proxy.
providers.note: Grok on your xAI bill.

providers.label: GitHub Copilot (your subscription)
providers.proxyHint: needs a Copilot-aware proxy + your GitHub auth; reaches GPT/Claude/Gemini behind Copilot.
providers.note: Models on your GitHub Copilot plan.

providers.label: OpenRouter (300+ models, your key)
providers.proxyHint: one key unlocks 300+ models; use OpenRouter's Anthropic-compatible endpoint or a translating proxy. Confirm the base URL.
providers.note: Hundreds of models on one OpenRouter bill. This single row is most of Hermes-style breadth.

providers.label: DeepSeek (your key)
providers.proxyHint: via a translating proxy; confirm the base URL + model ids.
providers.note: Strong and cheap, on your DeepSeek bill. Not Claude-grade on agentic tool use.

providers.label: Mistral (your key)
providers.proxyHint: OpenAI-shaped; via a translating proxy.
providers.note: EU-hosted models on your Mistral bill.

providers.label: Groq (fastest, your key)
providers.proxyHint: OpenAI-shaped; via a translating proxy. Famous for very high tokens/sec.
providers.note: Open models at top throughput on your Groq bill.

providers.label: Cerebras (fastest, your key)
providers.proxyHint: OpenAI-shaped; via a translating proxy. Wafer-scale inference, extreme speed.
providers.note: Open models at extreme throughput on your Cerebras bill.

providers.label: Fireworks AI (your key)
providers.proxyHint: OpenAI-shaped; via a translating proxy.
providers.note: Fast open-model serving on your Fireworks bill.

providers.label: Together AI (your key)
providers.proxyHint: OpenAI-shaped; via a translating proxy.
providers.note: A broad open-model catalog on your Together bill.

providers.label: Perplexity (your key)
providers.proxyHint: OpenAI-shaped; via a translating proxy. Sonar models include live web grounding.
providers.note: Web-grounded models on your Perplexity bill.

providers.label: NVIDIA NIM (free tier / your key)
providers.proxyHint: OpenAI-shaped; via a translating proxy. build.nvidia.com has a free tier.
providers.note: Nemotron + open models, free tier or your NVIDIA account.

providers.label: NovitaAI (200+ models, your key)
providers.proxyHint: OpenAI-shaped; via a translating proxy.
providers.note: A 200+ open-model catalog on your Novita bill.

providers.label: Hugging Face Inference (your key)
providers.proxyHint: OpenAI-shaped router across many open-model backends; via a translating proxy.
providers.note: Open models across providers on your HF token.

providers.label: Qwen / DashScope (your key)
providers.proxyHint: OpenAI-compatible mode; via a translating proxy.
providers.note: Alibaba Qwen models on your DashScope bill.

providers.label: Kimi / Moonshot (your key)
providers.proxyHint: OpenAI-shaped; via a translating proxy.
providers.note: Kimi long-context models on your Moonshot bill.

providers.label: GLM / Zhipu (your key)
providers.proxyHint: OpenAI-shaped; via a translating proxy.
providers.note: GLM models on your Zhipu bill.

providers.label: MiniMax (your key)
providers.proxyHint: OpenAI-shaped; via a translating proxy.
providers.note: MiniMax models on your bill.

providers.label: Ollama (local GPU)
providers.proxyHint: Ollama's /v1 is OpenAI-shaped; a translating proxy is usually needed. No prompt caching, no tool_choice.
providers.note: Nothing leaves the machine; $0; air-gapped. Weaker and slower than Claude.

providers.label: LM Studio (local GPU)
providers.proxyHint: OpenAI-shaped local server; via a translating proxy.
providers.note: Fully local. Same capability tradeoff as any local model.

providers.label: vLLM (local / self-host)
providers.proxyHint: vLLM silently fails tool-calls with the wrong --tool-call-parser; set it correctly. Via a translating proxy.
providers.note: Self-hosted throughput server. Tool reliability depends on the parser config.

providers.label: llama.cpp (local CPU/GPU)
providers.proxyHint: llama-server needs --jinja for tool calling; via a translating proxy.
providers.note: Runs on CPU, Metal, or a consumer GPU. Fully local.

providers.label: Jan (local desktop)
providers.proxyHint: OpenAI-shaped local server; via a translating proxy.
providers.note: A local desktop model runner. Fully local.

providers.label: claude-code-router (your config)
providers.proxyHint: start the router first (`ccr` / `ccr code`); it routes to whatever providers YOU configured.
providers.note: You run the router; Urfael points at 127.0.0.1. Multi-provider via one local hop.

providers.label: LiteLLM proxy (your config)
providers.proxyHint: start LiteLLM first; PIN a clean version. Routes to your configured providers (100+).
providers.note: You run + secure the proxy; Urfael points at 127.0.0.1.
